notes.anchor: dispatched on the Kalends of August
notes.note: 1 August 58 BC; the Kalends were the first day of the Roman month. Cicero's whole route hangs on the letters Atticus sent that day, which had not yet reached him.

notes.anchor: you are obliged both to console and to tell the truth
notes.note: Cicero's wry diagnosis of Atticus's letters: they raise a hope and then withdraw it, because Atticus is torn between comforting his friend and being honest about the bleak prospects of recall.

notes.anchor: Sent the twelfth day before the Kalends [of September]
notes.note: By inclusive Roman reckoning, the twelfth day before the Kalends (first) of September, i.e. 21 August 58 BC. The month is supplied editorially; the manuscripts give only "data xii Kal."
